turns.speaker: User
turns.text: Carbon taxes are economically efficient but politically impossible. Cap-and-trade has actually worked in the EU ETS after initial failures. Why does the US still resist market mechanisms entirely?

turns.speaker: Assistant
turns.text: The resistance is partly structural—US politics rewards short election cycles, and carbon pricing requires voters to accept near-term costs for diffuse future benefits. The EU ETS also nearly collapsed in 2013 due to oversupply.

turns.speaker: User
turns.text: Right, the Phase 1 permit giveaway problem. They learned and backloaded. Could a US version learn from those mistakes upfront?

scored_turn.speaker: User
scored_turn.text: Carbon taxes are economically efficient but politically impossible. Cap-and-trade has actually worked in the EU ETS after initial failures. Why does the US still resist market mechanisms entirely?

scores.rationale: Cites empirical evidence and draws comparative policy conclusions.
Acknowledges failure in EU system while still engaging constructively.
Policy-oriented framing suggests democratic problem-solving orientation.
